frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: LIP accumulates evidence with drift-diffusion dynamics until a bound is reached

frameworks.name: threshold/bound termination
frameworks.one_line: SC bursts implement a threshold sensor that terminates accumulated evidence

frameworks.name: bursting dynamics
frameworks.one_line: single-trial SC activity shows bursts distinct from gradual LIP buildup

core_question: Where and how is an accumulated decision variable read out to terminate a perceptual choice?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions arise from evidence accumulation to a bound
LIP carries the accumulated decision variable
termination requires a separable threshold-sensing mechanism
manipulations: simultaneous LIP and SC recordings during perceptual choice
single-trial dynamics analysis versus trial-averaged activity
key_findings: LIP exhibits drift-diffusion single-trial dynamics while SC exhibits bursting
trial-averaged activity in LIP and SC looks similar despite distinct single-trial dynamics
SC inactivation prolongs LIP buildup and impairs threshold-diagnostic behavior
interpretations: SC bursts implement the threshold readout that commits a decision
deliberation in LIP is transformed into commitment by an SC threshold mechanism
